models.model_label: RPG Ontology built on UFO/OntoUML for Role-Playing Games

evidence_quotes.quote: After exporting data from our model simulating individuals found in both games to the OWL format, a total of 2,233 triplets was generated resorting to the Triple-Store Apache Jena Fuseki [27] persistent triple store.
This allowed us to both syntactically and semantically validate our ontology using OWL tools.
Moreover, SPARQL queries were executed to respond to the previously enumerated competence questions.

goals.justification: The model was actively used to generate an OWL artifact (2,233 triplets) loaded into a triple store and queried via SPARQL. This matches the For Intervening signal of 'generated artifact' and the model directly enabling the creation of a working semantic implementation.

evidence_quotes.quote: This work aims to provide an improved semantic model, which is necessary to consult and discuss a given material in common for these RPG platforms.
In addition, the practices from IS and their criteria regarding planning are also considered in this first attempt to incorporate IS planning models [3] [21] to understand the actions and controls.

goals.justification: The paper frames the ontology as a means to clarify and conceptualize RPG domain elements (planning, system, scenario, entities) and explicitly states it is used 'to understand the actions and controls,' matching For Understanding positive signals (understand, conceptualize).

evidence_quotes.quote: This work aims to provide an improved semantic model, which is necessary to consult and discuss a given material in common for these RPG platforms.

uncertain_goals.reason: The phrase 'consult and discuss' hints at communication between parties, but the paper does not concretely describe meetings, stakeholder alignment, or live discussions; the evidence is indirect and could equally support Understanding or Documenting.

evidence_quotes.quote: Materials an important addendum to the proposed model. These could be game books, magazines, websites, and forums. These materials have records representing a myriad of elements from both RPG and MMORPG, including systems, scenarios, and even game matches.

uncertain_goals.reason: While the ontology references documentary materials as objects of representation, the paper does not explicitly frame the model itself as a documentation/knowledge-base resource for absent parties; the evidence is weak.
